title: Beach Sand Becomes Your Glass Bottle
style_notes: House style of SkyBlue/Mirror/HotDog: 1080x1920, FPS 30, single SVG layer per scene over a warm furnace-toned animated gradient background with drifting embers (like HotDog SPECK but orange). Energy from FAST in-composition motion (snappy springs via usePop damping 110-140 mass 0.55, fast spins, fills, morphs, balloon-inflate) NOT camera zoom pulses. Reuse shared Defs: glow + soft filters, plus gradients moltenG (radial yellow->orange->red), glassG (cyan glass), sandG (sandy), steelG (machinery). Each scene wrapped in SceneWrap (fast 8-frame fade + tiny 1.06->1 settle). Big bold 3-word karaoke captions auto-added at bottom 250 throughout (do not build). Label pill at top y=250 names each step. Scene timing must be driven by real per-word sentence-end timestamps in sandglass_words.json once narrated; the per-scene frame budgets below are the design target at 30fps.

scenes.beat: The glass bottle in your hand was once a pile of ordinary beach sand.
scenes.visual: A clean clear-glass bottle drawn with the glassG gradient (cyan tint, white rim highlight, soft drop shadow) sits centered. A label pill is NOT used here. The bottle's body is filled by a clip-path; inside it we see piled-up sand grains. Then a curiosity beat: the whole bottle dissolves grain-by-grain DOWN into a heap of beach sand on a thin beach-line at the bottom, with a tiny seashell and a sun-glint, literally answering 'this WAS sand'. A pulsing gold '?' glyph (like Mirror) sits at the bottle shoulder before the dissolve.
scenes.animations: Bottle springs up from scale 0.4 with overshoot (usePop damping 120). '?' pulses opacity 0.7->1 via sin. At ~frame 55 the bottle outline stays but its glass fill 'shatters' into ~120 sand-colored grain dots that fall + scatter via interpolated y with random x drift, settling into a sand pile (each grain delayed by index). A reverse-reveal: grains pour from bottle silhouette into the heap. Heat-shimmer is absent here (cool blue) to contrast with furnace scenes next.
scenes.sfx: soft glass-clink/chime as the bottle pops in (assets/sound_effects), then a granular sand-pour/whoosh as it dissolves into the heap (one dramatic-ish reveal cue).
scenes.onscreen_text: FROM THIS  →  was SAND  (small gold tag near the heap)

scenes.beat: First, that sand is mixed with crushed recycled glass and heated to nearly 3,000 degrees.
scenes.visual: A 'recipe' mixing step on the left feeding a glowing furnace mouth on the right. Two labeled streams pour into a steel hopper: a SAND stream (sandG colored grains) and a CRUSHED GLASS stream (cyan glassG shards, little angular polygons). They merge in the hopper, then slide right into an open arched furnace door that glows hotter and hotter. A big bold animated thermometer / temperature readout climbs to '~3000F'. Heat-shimmer wavy lines and orange glow ramp up. Label pill: 'Sand + recycled glass'.
scenes.animations: Two pour streams animate as falling-particle columns (HotDog FALL style) with fast index-staggered drops; grains tumble/rotate. Hopper auger-style churn mixes them (fast rotate). Furnace interior color mix()es from dark steel -> deep red -> bright orange as a 'heat' interpolate ramps; glow filter intensifies. Temperature number counts UP fast via interpolate (round) from 70 to 2912, with the digits popping. Shimmer = animated sine paths rising. A red '!' or flame-lick flickers at the door on the temperature reveal.
scenes.sfx: granular pour/shaker rattle on the two streams, then a low furnace WHOOSH / ignition roar as the door glow ramps and the temperature hits ~3000 (second reveal cue).

scenes.beat: Then it melts into a glowing orange blob, thick and gooey like honey.
scenes.visual: Centerpiece: a fat glowing molten-glass blob using a radial moltenG gradient (white-hot core -> orange -> deep red rim) with the glow filter, sitting in/oozing from a steel crucible lip. It visibly behaves like honey: a thick viscous drip stretches down and snaps back, slow gooey wobble of the blob outline. A floating 'like honey' comparison: a tiny honey dipper / drizzle icon next to it for the simile, with a side-by-side viscosity hint.
scenes.animations: Blob outline morphs via animated control points (gooey wobble using sin on a path 'd', SkyBlue-style path building) so it breathes and sags. A honey-thick drip elongates downward (interpolate length) then a droplet pinches and the strand recoils (spring). The molten gradient pulses brightness (core scales 1+0.03 sin like the Sun). Heat-shimmer + rising ember sparks. The honey-dipper icon does a slow drizzle of a matching glowing strand to sell 'gooey like honey'.
scenes.sfx: low bubbling/lava glug bed (subtle), plus a soft viscous 'stretch' squelch on the drip. No big boom here (keep SFX sparse).

scenes.beat: And a machine snips off a perfect droplet, dropping it straight into a bottle-shaped mold.
scenes.visual: Cross-section / process diagram: at the top, a molten strand (the 'gob') hangs from a steel feeder. Two steel shear blades (steelG) wait on either side. They SNIP, cutting a perfect glowing droplet (the 'gob') which then falls down a guide chute straight into an open bottle-shaped MOLD shown in cutaway below (two mold halves, hollow bottle-cavity outline visible). The gob lands and glows inside the cavity. Label pill: 'A glowing gob is cut'.
scenes.animations: Shear blades snap shut FAST (spring, the two halves translate toward center and overshoot) — the crisp snip is the beat's hero motion. At the instant they meet, the strand splits and a glowing droplet detaches and falls with accelerating y (gravity-eased) plus a slight squash on landing. Mold halves are pre-open; the gob drops through and settles glowing in the bottle cavity, lighting the cavity walls orange. A small 'snip' spark flash at the cut point.
scenes.sfx: a sharp metallic SNIP / scissor-clink exactly on the blade close (scene-matched click), then a soft 'plop' as the gob lands in the mold.
scenes.onscreen_text: PERFECT DROPLET  (gold, pops at the snip)

scenes.beat: Next, a blast of air inflates the blob from the inside, pressing it against the walls like a balloon.
scenes.visual: THE HERO REVEAL. Cutaway of the closed bottle mold (two steel mold halves outlined, steelG). A blow-pipe / air nozzle enters from the top. A burst of air (animated arrows + puff) shoots DOWN into the molten gob, and the glowing glass inflates outward from a small slug into the full bottle shape, pressing flush against the inner mold walls — exactly 'like a balloon'. Show the air pressure as expanding concentric rings + directional arrows pushing the glass to the walls. The glass goes from thick molten orange to thinning, brightening, taking the crisp bottle silhouette.
scenes.animations: Air-blast: nozzle pops in, then a fast puff of air-arrow particles fires downward (staggered). The glass body INFLATES via an interpolated scale/path-expansion from a small blob to the bottle outline, overshooting slightly then snapping to the mold walls (spring) — balloon stretch feel. Inner pressure rings expand and fade. As it inflates, color mix()es molten orange -> hot yellow at the thin walls. Tiny wall-contact 'tick' flashes where the glass kisses the mold edges. A balloon icon briefly morphs alongside to nail the simile.
scenes.sfx: an air-blast WHOOSH / pneumatic hiss on the blow, then a stretchy balloon-inflate creak rising to a soft 'pop'/thunk as it seats against the walls (the third real reveal — biggest cue, a satisfying boom/thunk).
scenes.onscreen_text: INFLATED LIKE A BALLOON  (gold, pops at full inflation)

scenes.beat: Finally, the bottle cools slowly so it doesn't crack, hardening back into the clear glass you toss away without a thought.
scenes.visual: The finished bottle is released from the mold (mold halves slide apart) glowing orange, then COOLS: its color sweeps top-to-bottom from molten orange -> deep red -> dim -> finally the clear cyan glassG of scene 1. A 'slow cooling' annealing meter / gentle thermometer ticks DOWN. A warning of cracking (a faint crack line that tries to appear) is prevented because it cools slowly — show one near-crack that heals. Payoff: the now-clear glass bottle is tossed (a small recycle/trash arc) and the closing line lands — 'you toss away without a thought', closing the loop to the opening bottle.
scenes.animations: Mold halves spring apart (translate out + fade). Cooling = a vertical color-sweep mask: a falling 'cool line' interpolates the body fill from molten -> clear glass (mix over time). Glow filter fades out as it hardens. A hairline crack briefly draws then retracts (interpolate stroke-dash) with a small 'no-crack' shield tick. Final clear bottle does a tiny arc toss (translate + rotate, spring) toward a recycle bin / off-frame, then the payoff text pops in (usePop) over a settling clear bottle. Loops visually back to the scene-1 clear bottle.
scenes.sfx: a gentle cooling hiss / metallic settle as colors fade, then a final light 'clink' + soft success sting on the closing payoff line.

payoff: Beach sand → 3000°F → your bottle. Tossed without a thought.

captions.note: Big bold 3-word karaoke pages (gold active word, scale pop) burned at bottom 250 from sandglass_words.json — handled automatically, not part of this plan.

sfx_policy: Sparse + scene-matched. Slides/whooshes on scene changes; only THREE dramatic cues across the whole short: (1) the sand-pour reveal in the hook, (2) the furnace ignition roar hitting ~3000°F, (3) the balloon-inflate pop/thunk when the glass seats against the mold walls. The snip is a crisp small click, not a boom. Final payoff gets a soft success sting only.
